items.markdown:
![Drought hurts quail population, but state maintains hunting ...](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/04282008-1118-0139-0000-9a5d0f558912:4)
For the 2025–2026 season, Arizona quail season (Gambel's, Scaled, and California) runs from **October 17, 2025, through February 8, 2026** . The specialized Mearns’ quail season opens later, running from **December 5, 2025, through February 8, 2026** . The daily bag limit is 15 in aggregate, with a possession limit of 45.[[1]](https://www.azgfd.com/2025/10/17/quail-outlook-a-challenging-season-ahead/)[[2]](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/small-game-forecast/)[[3]](https://arizonaquailhuntingcamp.weebly.com/2025-2026-quail-journal.html)

**Key Details for Arizona Quail Hunting:** 

- **Species:** Arizona offers hunting for Gambel’s (statewide), Scaled (eastern), Mearns’ (southern mountains), and California (valley) quail.
- **Season Dates:** 
	- **General Season (Gambel’s/Scaled/California):** Oct. 17, 2025 – Feb. 8, 2026.
	- **Mearns' Quail Season:** Dec. 5, 2025 – Feb. 8, 2026.
- **Limits:** Daily bag limit is 15 in total (no more than 8 can be Mearns'). Possession limit is 45 in total.
- **Requirements:** A valid Arizona hunting license is required.
- **Best Time:** While the season opens in October, serious Mearns' hunting typically begins in late November or December.[[1]](https://www.azgfd.com/2025/10/17/quail-outlook-a-challenging-season-ahead/)[[2]](https://projectupland.com/quail-subspecies/gambels-quail/bird-hunting-arizona/#:~:text=Arizona%20is%20known%20for%20its%20upland%20hunting%2C,December%201%2C%202023%20to%20February%2011%2C%202024.)[[3]](https://www.divebombindustries.com/blogs/news/quail-hunting-in-arizona-mearns-scaled-and-gambel-tips)[[4]](https://www.saquailforever.com/arizona-quail-hunting-resources/)[[5]](https://arizonaquailhuntingcamp.weebly.com/spring-2025.html)[[6]](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/small-game-forecast/)

It is highly recommended to check the official [Arizona Game & Fish Department website](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/quail-hunting-in-arizona/) for the most current regulations and annual updates.[[1]](https://www.divebombindustries.com/blogs/news/quail-hunting-in-arizona-mearns-scaled-and-gambel-tips)

items.title: Are there wild quail in Arizona?

expanded_element.title: Quail - Arizona Game & Fish Department
expanded_element.description: Quail hunters in Arizona spend the majority of their time pursuing three species of quail, Gambel's, scaled or Mearns'. A fourth, the California quail, receives little hunting pressure and is found along the Little Colorado River drainage near Springerville.

items.markdown:
Arizona dove season generally features an early season from **September 1–15** and a late season, typically running from late November to early January . The daily bag limit is 15 total, with a 45-bird possession limit (max 10 white-winged daily), while Eurasian collared-doves have no limits. A valid Arizona hunting license and a [Migratory Bird Stamp](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/dove-hunting-in-arizona/) are required for hunters aged 18+.[[1]](https://www.spragues.com/dove-hunting.html#:~:text=Other%20places%20to%20hunt%20dove%20include:%20*,4th%20*%20**Year%20round**%20Eurasian%20Collared%20Dove)[[2]](https://www.buckeyeaz.gov/residents/police/hunting-and-shooting)[[3]](https://www.visitgilabend.org/post/the-dove-hunt-is-on)

**Key Season Details** 

- **Early Season:** September 1st–15th.
- **Late Season:** November 21st–January 4th (typical).
- **Eurasian Collared-Dove:** Year-round with unlimited daily and possession limits.[[1]](https://www.facebook.com/azgfd/posts/early-dove-season-ends-today-sept-15-at-legal-sunset-what-were-some-of-your-favo/1173544198137983/#:~:text=Opening%20day%20of%20dove%20season%20is%20just,for%20what%20is%20hopefully%20another%20great%20hunt?)[[2]](https://www.spragues.com/dove-hunting.html#:~:text=Other%20places%20to%20hunt%20dove%20include:%20*,4th%20*%20**Year%20round**%20Eurasian%20Collared%20Dove)[[3]](https://www.visitgilabend.org/post/the-dove-hunt-is-on)[[4]](https://www.buckeyeaz.gov/residents/police/hunting-and-shooting)

**Regulations & Requirements** 

- **Limits:** Daily bag limit is 15, with a maximum of 10 white-winged doves per day.
- **Possession Limit:** 45 total doves after opening day.
- **License:** Hunters 18+ need an Arizona hunting license and a Migratory Bird Stamp.
- **Requirements:** Must participate in the Harvest Information Program (HIP).
- **Safety:** Do not shoot within 1/4 mile of an occupied structure, road, or railway.[[1]](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/dove-hunting-in-arizona/#:~:text=Here%20are%20some%20do's%20and%20don'ts%20for,violations%20to%20Operation%20Game%20Thief%2C%20800%20352-0700)[[2]](https://www.buckeyeaz.gov/residents/police/hunting-and-shooting)[[3]](https://www.visitgilabend.org/post/the-dove-hunt-is-on)

**Popular Hunting Areas** 

- **[Robbins Butte Wildlife Area](https://www.google.com/search?q=Robbins+Butte+Wildlife+Area&csui=3&hl=en&gl=US&uule=w+CAIQIFISCQs2MuSEtepUEUK33kOSuTsc&ved=2ahUKEwiX967ChJGUAxUiGTQIHcwiNSIQgK4QegYIAQgBEBA):** A popular spot near Buckeye.
- **[Powers Butte Wildlife Area](https://www.google.com/search?q=Powers+Butte+Wildlife+Area&csui=3&hl=en&gl=US&uule=w+CAIQIFISCQs2MuSEtepUEUK33kOSuTsc&ved=2ahUKEwiX967ChJGUAxUiGTQIHcwiNSIQgK4QegYIAQgBEBI):** Popular location in Buckeye.
- **[Arlington Wildlife Area](https://www.google.com/search?q=Arlington+Wildlife+Area&csui=3&hl=en&gl=US&uule=w+CAIQIFISCQs2MuSEtepUEUK33kOSuTsc&ved=2ahUKEwiX967ChJGUAxUiGTQIHcwiNSIQgK4QegYIAQgBEBQ):** Located in Buckeye.
- **[Yuma Area](https://www.google.com/search?q=Yuma+Area&csui=3&hl=en&gl=US&uule=w+CAIQIFISCQs2MuSEtepUEUK33kOSuTsc&ved=2ahUKEwiX967ChJGUAxUiGTQIHcwiNSIQgK4QegYIAQgBEBY):** Known as a premier location in southwest Arizona.[[1]](https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/1105cba27ddf4df797df35961e672608)[[2]](https://www.facebook.com/buckeyeaz.gov/videos/opening-day-of-dove-season-is-almost-herestarting-september-1-hunters-from-acros/649129774410533/)[[3]](https://www.pheasantsforever.org/BlogLanding/Blogs/Pheasants-Forever/A-Dove%E2%80%99s-Dream-in-the-Desert.aspx)

It is highly recommended to check for updates on the Arizona Game and Fish Department website before heading out.[[1]](https://www.azgfd.com/2023/07/11/dove-band-tailed-pigeon-regulations-online/#:~:text=All%20hunters%2018%20and%20older%20must%20possess,which%20can%20be%20purchased%20now%20at%20www.azgfd.com/license/.)[](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/dove-hunting-in-arizona/#:~:text=Here%20are%20some%20do's%20and%20don'ts%20for,violations%20to%20Operation%20Game%20Thief%2C%20800%20352-0700) Dove hunting in Arizona Here are some do's and don'ts for dove hunting: * Do not shoot within ¼ mile (440 yards) of an occupied structure without the resi...![](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/04282008-1118-0139-0000-a7ccb3ac7a66:0)
Arizona Game & Fish Department[](https://www.azgfd.com/2023/07/11/dove-band-tailed-pigeon-regulations-online/#:~:text=All%20hunters%2018%20and%20older%20must%20possess,which%20can%20be%20purchased%20now%20at%20www.azgfd.com/license/.) 2023-2024 dove, band-tailed pigeon regs online - Arizona Game & ...All hunters 18 and older must possess a valid Arizona hunting license, as well as a migratory bird stamp for the 2023-2024 season ...![](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/04282008-1118-0139-0000-a7ccb3ac7a66:1)

items.text: Arizona dove season generally features an early season from September 1–15 and a late season, typically running from late November to early January. The daily bag limit is 15 total, with a 45-bird possession limit (max 10 white-winged daily), while Eurasian collared-doves have no limits. A valid Arizona hunting license and a Migratory Bird Stamp are required for hunters aged 18+. Sprague's Sports +2
items.markdown: Arizona dove season generally features an early season from **September 1–15** and a late season, typically running from late November to early January . The daily bag limit is 15 total, with a 45-bird possession limit (max 10 white-winged daily), while Eurasian collared-doves have no limits. A valid Arizona hunting license and a [Migratory Bird Stamp](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/dove-hunting-in-arizona/) are required for hunters aged 18+.[[1]](https://www.spragues.com/dove-hunting.html#:~:text=Other%20places%20to%20hunt%20dove%20include:%20*,4th%20*%20**Year%20round**%20Eurasian%20Collared%20Dove)[[2]](https://www.buckeyeaz.gov/residents/police/hunting-and-shooting)[[3]](https://www.visitgilabend.org/post/the-dove-hunt-is-on)

items.title: Regulations & Requirements
items.text: Limits: Daily bag limit is 15, with a maximum of 10 white-winged doves per day. Possession Limit: 45 total doves after opening day. License: Hunters 18+ need an Arizona hunting license and a Migratory Bird Stamp. Migratory Bird Stamp Requirements: Must participate in the Harvest Information Program (HIP). Harvest Information Program (HIP) Safety: Do not shoot within 1/4 mile of an occupied structure, road, or railway.
items.markdown:
- **Limits:** Daily bag limit is 15, with a maximum of 10 white-winged doves per day.
- **Possession Limit:** 45 total doves after opening day.
- **License:** Hunters 18+ need an Arizona hunting license and a Migratory Bird Stamp.
- **Requirements:** Must participate in the Harvest Information Program (HIP).
- **Safety:** Do not shoot within 1/4 mile of an occupied structure, road, or railway.[[1]](https://www.azgfd.com/hunting/hunt-draw-and-licenses/species-hunt-information/dove-hunting-in-arizona/#:~:text=Here%20are%20some%20do's%20and%20don'ts%20for,violations%20to%20Operation%20Game%20Thief%2C%20800%20352-0700)[[2]](https://www.buckeyeaz.gov/residents/police/hunting-and-shooting)[[3]](https://www.visitgilabend.org/post/the-dove-hunt-is-on)

items.text: Robbins Butte Wildlife Area : A popular spot near Buckeye. Powers Butte Wildlife Area : Popular location in Buckeye. Arlington Wildlife Area : Located in Buckeye. Yuma Area : Known as a premier location in southwest Arizona.

items.title: What are the odds on Coues deer draw in Arizona?
Is 6 shot too big for dove?

expanded_element.title: What Chokes and Shot Size to Use for Dove Hunting - Project Upland
expanded_element.description: 7 or even 6 depending on the conditions. The smaller No. 8 pellets are perfect for mourning doves, while 6, 7, or 7 1/2, make good on knocking down the larger white-winged doves and Eurasian collared doves. Hunters carrying a light 20- or 16-gauge should consider a 7/8 load, but a 1-ounce load of shot may fare better.

items.title: Where can I hunt doves in Arizona?

expanded_element.title: Where to Dove Hunt in Arizona: Your Ultimate Guide to a Thrilling ...
expanded_element.description:
Cibola National Wildlife Refuge
 This refuge, located just outside Yuma, offers designated hunting areas for dove hunting. The Cibola NWR is known for its large populations of doves and provides excellent access to hunters.
